title: query bug racecondition
description:
A query should be returning a value but it's not.
If I change almost anything in the query, it starts working.
How to repeat:
CREATE TABLE temp (createdAt datetime, timezone varchar(64));

INSERT INTO temp VALUES ('2022-02-15 16:47:12', null);

SELECT
  *
FROM
  (
    SELECT
      coalesce(timezone, '+00:00') tz,
      convert_tz(createdAt, '+00:00', (select tz)) ca,
      if(createdAt, if((select 1) = 1, 1, 1), 1) random
    FROM temp
  ) aux
   WHERE ca = '2022-02-15 16:47:12';
Suggested fix:
I have no idea what is going on.. I imagine some race conditions 🤷‍♂️
poc: CREATE TABLE temp (createdAt datetime, timezone varchar(64));

SELECT
*
FROM
(
SELECT
coalesce(timezone, '+00:00') tz,
convert_tz(createdAt, '+00:00', (select tz)) ca,
if(createdAt, if((select 1) = 1, 1, 1), 1) random
FROM temp
) aux
WHERE ca = '2022-02-15 16:47:12';
key_bug_features: Query returns no value due to race condition
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE temp2 (id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY, createdAt DATETIME NOT NULL, timezone VARCHAR(64) DEFAULT '+00:00');
INSERT INTO temp2 (createdAt, timezone) VALUES ('2022-02-15 16:47:12', NULL), ('2022-02-15 16:47:12', '+05:00');
SELECT * FROM (SELECT coalesce(timezone, '+00:00') AS tz, convert_tz(createdAt, '+00:00', (SELECT tz FROM temp2 WHERE id = 1)) AS ca FROM temp2) AS aux WHERE ca = '2022-02-15 16:47:12';
CREATE TABLE temp3 (user_id INT, last_login DATETIME, status ENUM('active', 'inactive') DEFAULT 'active');
INSERT INTO temp3 (user_id, last_login) VALUES (1, '2022-02-15 16:47:12'), (2, NULL);
